against ARMOR endpoint\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, endpoint: str, access_key: str, secret_key: str, region: str = 'us-east-1'):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the verifier.\n\n        Args:\n            endpoint: ARMOR endpoint URL (e.g., http://localhost:9000)\n            access_key: ARMOR access key\n            secret_key: ARMOR secret key\n            region: AWS region (default: us-east-1)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.endpoint = endpoint.rstrip('/')\n        self.access_key = access_key\n        self.secret_key = secret_key\n        self.region = region\n        self.session = requests.Session()\n\n    def check_health(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if ARMOR endpoint is healthy\"\"\"\n        try:\n            response = self.session.get(f\"{self.endpoint}/healthz\", timeout=5)\n            if response.status_code == 200:\n                print_success(\"ARMOR health check passed\")\n                return True\n            else:\n                print_failure(f\"ARMOR health check failed: HTTP {response.status_code}\")\n                return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"ARMOR health check failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def verify_list_buckets(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify ListBuckets operation returns HTTP 200 with valid bucket list\n\n        Tests:\n        - HTTP 200 status code\n        - Valid XML response structure\n        - Required fields (Owner, Buckets)\n        - Proper S3 namespace\n        \"\"\"\n        print_header(\"Testing ListBuckets Operation\")\n\n        try:\n            # Make ListBuckets request\n            url = f\"{self.endpoint}/\"\n            response = self.session.get(url, timeout=10)\n\n            # Check status code\n            if response.status_code != 200:\n                print_failure(f\"ListBuckets returned HTTP {response.status_code}, expected 200\")\n                return False\n            print_success(\"ListBuckets returned HTTP 200\")\n\n            # Parse XML response\n            try:\n                root = ET.fromstring(response.content)\n                namespace = {'s3': 'http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/'}\n\n                # Check for required elements\n                owner = root.find('.//s3:Owner', namespace)\n                buckets = root.find('.//s3:Buckets', namespace)\n\n                if owner is None:\n                    print_failure(\"ListBuckets response missing Owner element\")\n                    return False\n                print_success(\"ListBuckets response has Owner element\")\n\n                if buckets is None:\n                    print_failure(\"ListBuckets response missing Buckets element\")\n                    return False\n                print_success(\"ListBuckets response has Buckets element\")\n\n                # Check Owner fields\n                owner_id = owner.find('s3:ID', namespace)\n                display_name = owner.find('s3:DisplayName', namespace)\n\n                if owner_id is not None:\n                    print_success(f\"Owner ID: {owner_id.text}\")\n                if display_name is not None:\n                    print_success(f\"Display Name: {display_name.text}\")\n\n                # Check if there are any buckets\n                bucket_elements = root.findall('.//s3:Bucket', namespace)\n                if bucket_elements:\n                    print_success(f\"Found {len(bucket_elements)} bucket(s)\")\n                    for bucket in bucket_elements[:3]:  # Show first 3\n                        name = bucket.find('s3:Name', namespace)\n                        if name is not None:\n                            print_info(f\"  - Bucket: {name.text}\")\n                else:\n                    print_info(\"No buckets found (empty list is valid)\")\n\n                return True\n\n            except ET.ParseError as e:\n                print_failure(f\"Failed to parse ListBuckets XML response: {e}\")\n                print_info(f\"Response body: {response.text[:200]}\")\n                return False\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"ListBuckets request failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def verify_head_object(self, bucket: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify HeadObject returns proper metadata\n\n        Tests:\n        - HTTP 200 or 404 status code (200 if object exists, 404 if not)\n        - Proper metadata headers when object exists\n        - Proper error response when object doesn't exist\n        \"\"\"\n        print_header(\"Testing HeadObject Operation\")\n\n        try:\n            # Test with a non-existent object (should get 404)\n            test_key = \"nonexistent-test-object-12345.txt\"\n            url = f\"{self.endpoint}/{bucket}/{test_key}\"\n            response = self.session.head(url, timeout=10)\n\n            # We expect either 200 (if object exists) or 404 (if not)\n            if response.status_code not in [200, 404]:\n                print_failure(f\"HeadObject returned HTTP {response.status_code}, expected 200 or 404\")\n                return False\n\n            if response.status_code == 404:\n                print_success(\"HeadObject returned HTTP 404 for non-existent object (expected)\")\n                # Verify error response structure\n                return self.verify_error_response(response, \"NoSuchKey\")\n            else:\n                print_success(\"HeadObject returned HTTP 200\")\n\n                # Check for S3 metadata headers\n                important_headers = [\n                    'Content-Length',\n                    'Content-Type',\n                    'ETag',\n                    'Last-Modified'\n                ]\n\n                found_headers = []\n                for header in important_headers:\n                    if header in response.headers:\n                        found_headers.append(header)\n                        print_success(f\"  {header}: {response.headers[header]}\")\n\n                if len(found_headers) >= 2:\n                    print_success(f\"HeadObject returned {len(found_headers)} metadata headers\")\n                    return True\n                else:\n                    print_warning(f\"HeadObject returned only {len(found_headers)} metadata headers\")\n                    return True  # Still pass, just fewer headers\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"HeadObject request failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def verify_list_objects_v2(self, bucket: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify ListObjectsV2 returns object listings\n\n        Tests:\n        - HTTP 200 status code\n        - Valid XML response structure\n        - Required fields (Name, Contents/CommonPrefixes)\n        - Proper object metadata in Contents\n        \"\"\"\n        print_header(\"Testing ListObjectsV2 Operation\")\n\n        try:\n            # Make ListObjectsV2 request\n            url = f\"{self.endpoint}/{bucket}/?list-type=2\"\n            response = self.session.get(url, timeout=10)\n\n            # Check status code\n            if response.status_code not in [200, 404]:\n                print_failure(f\"ListObjectsV2 returned HTTP {response.status_code}, expected 200 or 404\")\n                return False\n\n            if response.status_code == 404:\n                print_success(\"ListObjectsV2 returned HTTP 404 (bucket may not exist)\")\n                # Verify error response\n                return self.verify_error_response(response, \"NoSuchBucket\")\n\n            print_success(\"ListObjectsV2 returned HTTP 200\")\n\n            # Parse XML response\n            try:\n                root = ET.fromstring(response.content)\n                namespace = {'s3': 'http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/'}\n\n                # Check for required elements\n                name = root.find('s3:Name', namespace)\n                if name is None:\n                    print_failure(\"ListObjectsV2 response missing Name element\")\n                    return False\n                print_success(f\"ListObjectsV2 response for bucket: {name.text}\")\n\n                # Check Contents\n                contents = root.findall('.//s3:Contents', namespace)\n                if contents:\n                    print_success(f\"Found {len(contents)} object(s)\")\n                    for content in contents[:3]:  # Show first 3\n                        key = content.find('s3:Key', namespace)\n                        size = content.find('s3:Size', namespace)\n                        if key is not None:\n                            size_val = size.text if size is not None else \"0\"\n                            print_info(f\"  - {key.text} ({size_val} bytes)\")\n                else:\n                    print_info(\"No objects found (empty list is valid)\")\n\n                # Check CommonPrefixes (for directory-like structure)\n                common_prefixes = root.findall('.//s3:CommonPrefixes', namespace)\n                if common_prefixes:\n                    print_success(f\"Found {len(common_prefixes)} common prefix(es)\")\n\n                return True\n\n            except ET.ParseError as e:\n                print_failure(f\"Failed to parse ListObjectsV2 XML response: {e}\")\n                print_info(f\"Response body: {response.text[:200]}\")\n                return False\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"ListObjectsV2 request failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def verify_error_response(self, response, expected_error_code: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify error response has proper S3 error structure\n\n        Args:\n            response: HTTP response object\n            expected_error_code: Optional expected error code to check\n\n        Returns:\n            True if error response structure is valid\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Parse error XML\n            root = ET.fromstring(response.content)\n            namespace = {'s3': 'http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/'}\n\n            # Check for error elements\n            error = root.find('.//s3:Code', namespace)\n            message = root.find('.//s3:Message', namespace)\n\n            if error is None:\n                print_failure(\"Error response missing Code element\")\n                return False\n            print_success(f\"Error response has Code: {error.text}\")\n\n            if message is None:\n                print_failure(\"Error response missing Message element\")\n                return False\n            print_success(f\"Error response has Message: {message.text}\")\n\n            # Check expected error code\n            if expected_error_code and error.text != expected_error_code:\n                print_warning(f\"Expected error code {expected_error_code}, got {error.text}\")\n\n            return True\n\n        except ET.ParseError as e:\n            print_failure(f\"Failed to parse error XML response: {e}\")\n            print_info(f\"Response body: {response.text[:200]}\")\n            return False\n\n    def verify_error_operations(self, bucket: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify error operations return appropriate S3 error responses\n\n        Tests:\n        - Access to non-existent bucket returns NoSuchBucket\n        - Access to non-existent object returns NoSuchKey\n        - Invalid operations return proper error codes\n        \"\"\"\n        print_header(\"Testing Error Operations\")\n\n        all_passed = True\n\n        # Test 1: Access non-existent bucket\n        print_info(\"Test 1: Accessing non-existent bucket...\")\n        try:\n            fake_bucket = \"nonexistent-test-bucket-12345\"\n            url = f\"{self.endpoint}/{fake_bucket}/?list-type=2\"\n            response = self.session.get(url, timeout=10)\n\n        print_success(\"Error response has correct structure\")\n                else:\n                    all_passed = False\n            else:\n                print_warning(f\"Expected HTTP 404, got {response.status_code}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"Non-existent bucket test failed: {e}\")\n            all_passed = False\n\n        # Test 2: Access non-existent object\n        print_info(\"Test 2: Accessing non-existent object...\")\n        try:\n            fake_key = \"nonexistent-test-object-12345.txt\"\n            url = returned HTTP 404\")\n                if self.verify_error_response(response, \"NoSuchKey\"):\n                    print_success(\"Error response has correct structure\")\n                else:\n                    all_passed = False\n            else:\n                print_warning(f\"Expected HTTP 404, got {response.status_code}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"Non-existent object test failed: {e}\")\n            all_passed = False\n\n        # Test 3: Invalid S3 operation\n        print_info(\"Test 3: Testing invalid S3 operation...\")\n        try:\n            # Try to use POST on bucket (should get method not allowed or similar)\n            url = f\"{self.endpoint}/{bucket}/\"\n            response = self.session.post(url, data=\"test\", timeout=10)\n\n            if response.status_code >= 400:\n                print_success(f\"Invalid operation returned HTTP {response.status_code}\")\n                if self.verify_error_response(response):\n                    print_success(\"Error response has correct structure\")\n                else:\n                    all_passed = False\n            else:\n                print_warning(f\"Expected error status code, got {response.status_code}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            print_failure(f\"Invalid operation test failed: {e}\")\n            all_passed = False\n\n        return all_passed\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main verification runner\"\"\"\n    print_header(\"ARMOR Basic S3 Operations Verification\")\n    print(\"Bead: bf-58ri5x\")\n authentication.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport hashlib\nimport hmac\nimport datetime\nimport urllib.request\nimport urllib.parse\nimport xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\n\n# ANSI colors\nGREEN = '\\033[92m'\nRED = '\\033[91m'\nYELLOW = '\\033[93m'\nBLUE = '\\033[94m'\nRESET = '\\033[0m'\n\ndef print_success(msg):\n    print(f\"{GREEN}✓{RESET} {msg}\")\n\ndef print_failure(msg):\n    print(f\"{RED}✗{RESET} {msg}\")\n\ndef print_info(msg):\n    print(f\"{BLUE}ℹ{RESET} {msg}\")\n\ndef print_header(msg):\n    print(f\"\\n{BLUE}{'=' * 70}{RESET}\")\n    print(f\"{BLUE}{msg}{RESET}\")\n    print(f\"{BLUE}{'=' * 70}{RESET}\\n\")\n\nclass AWSV4Signer:\n    \"\"\"AWS Signature Version 4 signer\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, access_key, secret_key, region=\"us-east-005\"):\n        self.access_key = access_key\n        self.secret_key = secret_key\n        self.region = region\n        self.service = \"s3\"\n\n    def _sign(self, key, msg):\n        \"\"\"HMAC SHA256\"\"\"\n        return hmac.new(key, msg.encode('utf-8'), hashlib.sha256).digest()\n\n    def _get_signature_key(self, date_stamp):\n        \"\"\"Derive signature key\"\"\"\n        k_date = self._sign(('AWS4' + self.secret_key).encode('utf-8'), date_stamp)\n        k_region = self._sign(k_date, self.region)\n        k_service = self._sign(k_region, self.service)\n        k_signing = self._sign(k_service, 'aws4_request')\n        return k_signing\n\n    def sign_request(self, method, host, path, headers=None, body=b''):\n        \"\"\"Sign an HTTP request with AWS V4 signature\"\"\"\n        now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)\n        amz_date = now.strftime('%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ')\n        date_stamp = now.strftime('%Y%m%d')\n\n        if headers is None:\n            headers = {}\n\n        headers['X-Amz-Date'] = amz_date\n        if 'Host' not in headers:\n            headers['Host'] = host\n\n        payload_hash = key in sorted(headers.keys()):\n            lower_key = key.lower()\n            signed_headers_list.append(lower_key)\n            value = str(headers[key]).strip()\n            canonical_headers += f'{lower_key}:{value}\\n'\n\n        signed_headers = ';'.join(signed_headers_list)\n\n        # Canonical request\n        canonical_uri = urllib.parse.quote(path, safe='/')\n sign\n        credential_scope = f'{date_stamp}/{self.region}/{self.service}/aws4_request'\n        canonical_request_hash = hashlib.sha256(canonical_request.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n        string_to_sign = f'AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\\n{amz_date}\\n{credential_scope}\\n{canonical_request_hash}'\n\n        # Calculate signature\n        signing_key = self._get_signature_key(date_stamp)\n from urllib.parse import urlparse\n\n    parsed = urlparse(endpoint)\n    host = parsed.netloc or parsed.path\n    if ':' in host:\n        host = host.split(':')[0]\n\n    signer =
